markdown:
#  Unmark a Drawing as Obsolete 
##  Things to Consider 
  * **Required User Permissions:**
    * 'Admin' permissions on the project's Drawing tool.
  * **Prerequisites:**
    * [Mark a Drawing as Obsolete](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/drawings-project/tutorials/mark-drawings-as-obsolete)


##  Steps 
  1. Navigate to the project's **Drawings** tool.
  2. If your project uses [drawing areas](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-are-drawing-areas), you must select an area.
  3. Click **Reports** , select **All Sets and Revisions** from the drop down menu.
  4. Locate the obsolete drawing in the list.   
_Note_ : Obsolete drawings will appear as gray and italicized.
  5. Click the revision number you want to open for the obsolete drawing.   
_Note_ : This will open the drawing's General Information page, where all versions of the drawing will be listed.
  6. Click **Edit**.
  7. Clear the checkbox next to **Obsolete**.
  8. Click **Update**.  
The drawing will now appear in the drawing set where it was originally uploaded.


## See Also
  * [Mark Drawings as Obsolete](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/drawings-project/tutorials/mark-drawings-as-obsolete)
  * [What is the unpublished status on drawings?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-is-the-unpublished-status-on-drawings)
  * [View the All Sets and Revisions Report in the Drawings Tool](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/drawings-project/tutorials/view-the-all-sets-and-revisions-report-in-the-drawings-tool)
title: Unmark a Drawing as Obsolete
description: How to unmark a drawing as obsolete and return it to the current set of drawings.
